name: Alta Via 1
keyFeatures: Dolomite towers, via ferratas, Italian culture
costNotes: Rifugio beds ~€40–65/night incl. dinner & breakfast. No permit fee.

name: Walker's Haute Route
keyFeatures: Classic Alpine traverse, Matterhorn, glaciers
costNotes: Swiss/French Alpine huts €50–90/night. Swiss costs noticeably higher than French side.

name: Annapurna Base Camp (ABC)
keyFeatures: Mountain amphitheater, Gurung villages, close peaks
costNotes: Teahouse bed+board ~$15–30/night. ACAP & TIMS permits ~$50 total.

name: Annapurna Circuit
keyFeatures: Thorong La pass, Buddhist culture, diverse zones
costNotes: Teahouses avg $20–35/day all-in. ACAP + TIMS permits ~$50.

name: Camino de Santiago
keyFeatures: Pilgrimage route, medieval towns, cultural heritage
costNotes: Albergues €10–18/night. Budget €25–45/day all-in. One of Europe's best-value long walks.

name: Drakensberg Grand Traverse
keyFeatures: Escarpment ridge, San rock art, mountain passes
costNotes: Park entry ~R180/day. No staffed huts — bring all food. Extremely affordable.

name: Everest Base Camp
keyFeatures: Kala Patthar, Khumbu Icefall, Sherpa culture
costNotes: Teahouses $20–40/night. Sagarmatha NP + TIMS ~$100. Costs rise sharply closer to EBC.

name: GR20
keyFeatures: Cirque de la Solitude, granite peaks, vertical gain
costNotes: Refuges €15–20/night dormitory. Full board option ~€40–55. Camping cheaper at €8–12.

name: Huayhuash Circuit
keyFeatures: Yerupajá, Siula Grande, turquoise lakes
costNotes: Community entry fees ~$50 total. Fully self-sufficient camping required independently.

name: Inca Trail
keyFeatures: Machu Picchu, Dead Woman's Pass, Incan ruins
costNotes: Classic trail legally requires a licensed guide. Budget tour ~$700 pp, luxury $1800+.

name: John Muir Trail
keyFeatures: Mount Whitney, Ansel Adams Wilderness, granite peaks
costNotes: Self-sufficient — resupply packages at Muir Trail Ranch ~$200. Permit fees ~$15. Gear dominates cost.

name: K2 Base Camp
keyFeatures: K2, granite spires, massive glaciers
costNotes: Remote Karakoram — requires tents, porters & cook for most trekkers. Porter fees ~$25/day each.

name: Kilimanjaro (Machame)
keyFeatures: Uhuru Peak, Barranco Wall, ecological zones
costNotes: Guide mandatory by law. Park fees ~$800+. Budget operators ~$1800; reputable mid-range ~$3000.

name: Kungsleden
keyFeatures: Kebnekaise, Arctic wilderness, Sami culture
costNotes: STF hut beds ~SEK 320–450/night. Ferry crossings ~SEK 100 each. Resupply possible at STF stations.

name: Laugavegur Trail
keyFeatures: Rhyolite mountains, obsidian fields, hot springs
costNotes: Hut beds ~$70–100/night. Iceland is expensive — budget $80–120/day. Camping option ~$30/night.

name: Milford Track
keyFeatures: Mackinnon Pass, Sutherland Falls, Milford Sound
costNotes: Hut fees NZD220/night. Independent capacity strictly limited — book months ahead.

name: Mount Fuji
keyFeatures: Sunrise at summit, volcanic cone, cultural icon
costNotes: Mountain hut incl. dinner & breakfast ~¥15,000. Conservation fee ¥2,000. Very accessible.

name: Mount Kailash Kora
keyFeatures: Drolma La pass, spiritual pilgrimage, sacred peak
costNotes: Tibet Entry + Alien Travel Permit + group tour required. Logistics make independent travel near-impossible.

name: Overland Track
keyFeatures: Cradle Mountain, Lake St Clair, ancient pines
costNotes: Hut fees AUD$75/night peak season. Cradle Mountain park entry ~AUD$30. Self-guided is very doable.

name: Routeburn Track
keyFeatures: Harris Saddle, Southern Alps views, temperate forest
costNotes: Hut fees ~NZD$75/night. One of NZ's Great Walks — book huts months ahead in peak season.

name: Snowman Trek
keyFeatures: Lunana region, high passes, isolated valleys
costNotes: Bhutan mandatory $200/day Sustainable Development Fee + agency required. Guide-only trek.

name: Torres del Paine (O)
keyFeatures: Granite towers, glaciers, wildlife
costNotes: Park entry ~$40. Camping/refugios $25–80/night. Budget carefully — booking mandatory.

name: Tour du Mont Blanc
keyFeatures: Mont Blanc massif, three countries, alpine culture
costNotes: Refuges €50–80/night incl. dinner. French/Italian refuges cheaper than Swiss. One of Europe's most expensive treks.

name: West Coast Trail
keyFeatures: Ladders, suspension bridges, tide pools
costNotes: Permit CAD$130. Water taxi (Gordon River) ~CAD$30. Self-sufficient camping with all food carried.

name: Alta Via 2
keyFeatures: Remote Dolomites, Marmolada glacier, high passes
costNotes: Rifugio bed+board €50–70/night. More remote than AV1 — less competition for beds off peak.

name: Alta Via 4
keyFeatures: Less-traveled route, limestone formations, valleys
costNotes: Smaller rifugios €40–60/night. Less-traveled Dolomite route — often cheaper than the famous variants.

name: Arctic Circle Trail
keyFeatures: Remote Arctic, ice sheet views, tundra wildlife
costNotes: Remote Greenland — boat transfer ~$200 each end. Huts booked via Visit Greenland. Food must be carried.

name: Ausangate Circuit
keyFeatures: Rainbow mountains, sacred peak, colored lakes
costNotes: Basic arrieros (muleteers) & cook included on most guided trips. Independent requires navigation skill.

name: Banff Highline
keyFeatures: Canadian Rockies, alpine meadows, wildlife
costNotes: Backcountry permit ~CAD$10/night. All food carried. Bear canister required (hire ~CAD$50).

name: Coast to Coast
keyFeatures: Lake District, Yorkshire Dales, three coasts
costNotes: B&Bs £50–100/night. Budget £60–90/day. Guided self-catered groups notably cheaper than full service.

name: East Coast Trail
keyFeatures: Atlantic cliffs, seabirds, icebergs, fishing villages
costNotes: B&Bs CAD$80–150/night in fishing villages. Trail access is free. Very manageable solo or guided.

name: Fisherman's Trail
keyFeatures: Atlantic coast, fishing villages, beaches
costNotes: Guesthouses €40–70/night. Excellent budget coastal option — cheap food and accommodation.

name: Fitz Roy Trek
keyFeatures: Granite spires, glacial lakes, Patagonian wildlife
costNotes: Park entry ~$25. El Chaltén has great affordable hostels ~$20–40/night. Argentina offers excellent value.

name: Gokyo Lakes
keyFeatures: Turquoise lakes, Gokyo Ri viewpoint, glaciers
costNotes: Sagarmatha NP + TIMS ~$100. Teahouses $25–45/night all-in. Costs rise above 4500m.

name: West Highland Way
keyFeatures: Lochs, glens, Highland culture
costNotes: B&Bs/bunkhouses £30–80/night. Baggage transfer services add ~£150–200. Popular trail well served by budget options.

name: Jordan Trail
keyFeatures: Dana Biosphere Reserve, Nabataean ruins, sandstone canyons, Petra approach
costNotes: Dana to Petra section most popular. Wild camping permitted. Jordan Pass covers entry to many sites.

name: Kalalau Trail
keyFeatures: Na Pali Coast, sea cliffs, tropical valleys
costNotes: Permit required ~$15/night. Kauai food/accommodation outside trail adds up. Trail itself very cheap.

name: Kumano Kodo
keyFeatures: Ancient pilgrimage, sacred shrines, cedar forests
costNotes: Ryokans ¥10,000–20,000/night incl. dinner & breakfast. Pilgrimage stamp book (nōkyōchō) a wonderful memento.

name: Larapinta Trail
keyFeatures: Red desert, Aboriginal culture, waterholes
costNotes: Trail registration AUD$20. Water cache service AUD$300+. Guided luxury camping tours ~AUD$4000.

name: Lycian Way
keyFeatures: Mediterranean coast, ancient ruins, beaches
costNotes: Pensions €15–30/night. Turkey extremely good value — food and accommodation cheap by European standards.

name: Manaslu Circuit
keyFeatures: Remote region, Tibetan culture, Larkya La pass
costNotes: Restricted Area Permit ~$100/week. Teahouses cheaper than Everest region. Camping required some sections.

name: Markha Valley
keyFeatures: Ladakhi culture, Buddhist monasteries, desert landscape
costNotes: Homestays ~₹500–1200/night incl. meals. Inner Line Permit required (~₹400). One of India's best-value treks.

name: Mount Kenya
keyFeatures: Equatorial glaciers, afroalpine flora, volcanic peaks
costNotes: Park entry ~$60/day. Mountain huts ~$20–30/night. Guide + porter strongly advised. Budget airlines from Nairobi.

name: Peaks of the Balkans
keyFeatures: Three countries, traditional villages, remote peaks
costNotes: Guesthouses €20–40/night incl. meals. Albania/Kosovo extremely cheap by European standards.

name: Salkantay Trek
keyFeatures: Glacial pass, cloud forest, Machu Picchu
costNotes: Budget agencies ~$250–400 all-inclusive. Luxury lodge option ~$1000+. One of Peru's best-value routes.

name: Simien Mountains
keyFeatures: Gelada baboons, dramatic escarpment, endemic wildlife
costNotes: Guide + scout mandatory by law ~$50/day total. Camp fees ~$5/night. Affordable once in Ethiopia.

name: Tiger Leaping Gorge
keyFeatures: Deep gorge, Jade Dragon Snow Mountain, cliff trails
costNotes: Guesthouses ¥60–150/night. One of the world's best-value iconic walks — 2 days, very cheap.

name: Tongariro Circuit
keyFeatures: Volcanic craters, emerald lakes, active geothermal
costNotes: Hut/camping fees ~NZD$30–55/night. Day-walk (Alpine Crossing) free entry. Great value NZ circuit.

name: Triglav Circuit
keyFeatures: Julian Alps, karst formations, Slovenia's highest peak
costNotes: Mountain huts €30–50/night incl. dinner. Triglav National Park entry free. Slovenia excellent value.

name: Wonderland Trail
keyFeatures: Mount Rainier circuit, glaciers, wildflower meadows
costNotes: NPS permit ~$30. Self-sufficient camping — no resupply. Mount Rainier NP annual pass ~$55.

name: Acatenango-Fuego
keyFeatures: Erupting Volcán de Fuego, volcanic summits, cloud forest
costNotes: Guided overnight ~$100 all-in from Antigua. One of the world's best budget volcano experiences.

name: Ala-Kul Lake Trek
keyFeatures: Turquoise Ala-Kul lake, Altyn-Arashan hot springs, high passes
costNotes: Yurt homestays ~$15–25/night incl. meals. Guide/horse hire ~$30/day. Extremely affordable Central Asia.

name: Altai Mountains
keyFeatures: Siberian wilderness, nomadic culture, remote peaks
costNotes: Requires guide and permits in border zones. Yurt stays ~₽1500–2500/night. Remote logistics add cost.

name: Caucasus (Tusheti)
keyFeatures: Defensive towers, shepherds, isolated region
costNotes: Guesthouses with meals ~₾50–80/night. Georgia extremely affordable. 4WD taxi to Omalo ~₾200.

name: Cordillera Real
keyFeatures: Bolivian peaks, Aymara culture, extreme altitude
costNotes: Bolivia one of South America's cheapest destinations. Refugios ~Bs.30–60. Guide/mule support cheap.

name: El Choro Trek
keyFeatures: Pre-Columbian trail, ecological transition, cloud forest
costNotes: Basic campsites ~$3–5. Rustic lodges possible. La Paz is the gateway — very cheap city to base from.

name: Fish River Canyon
keyFeatures: Ancient canyon, desert landscape, hot springs
costNotes: Namibia Wildlife Resorts permit ~N$420. Self-sufficient camping — all water carried. Fly-in to Ai-Ais adds cost.

name: Huemul Circuit
keyFeatures: Technical sections, remote wilderness, glacier crossings
costNotes: Technical route — river crossings, rope work. El Chaltén is the base. Argentina affordable for foreigners.

name: Lost City Trek
keyFeatures: Archaeological site, indigenous culture, rainforest
costNotes: Guide-only trek mandatory. Budget agencies ~$350 pp. Colombia very affordable overall.

name: Mestia to Ushguli
keyFeatures: Svan towers, Caucasus peaks, medieval villages
costNotes: Mestia guesthouses ~₾50–80 incl. meals. 4WD taxis available. One of Europe's best-value mountain areas.

name: Mgoun Traverse
keyFeatures: High Atlas, Berber culture, gorges
costNotes: Guide + mule recommended. Berber villages offer simple homestay meals ~DH100. Very affordable Atlas route.

name: Mount Rinjani
keyFeatures: Crater lake, active volcano, island views
costNotes: Guided packages from Senaru ~$250–500. National park entry ~Rp150,000. Very affordable Indonesia.

name: Rwenzori Mountains
keyFeatures: Equatorial glaciers, giant flora, biodiversity
costNotes: Guide + porter mandatory. Park fees ~$700+ for full circuit. Remote Uganda logistics add cost.

name: Santa Cruz Trek
keyFeatures: Cordillera Blanca, turquoise lakes, ice peaks
costNotes: Huascarán NP entry ~$20. Huaraz agencies offer budget 4-day packages ~$150–300. Excellent Peru value.

name: Mount Hood Timberline
keyFeatures: Pacific Northwest, glaciers, old-growth forest
costNotes: Mt Hood NF permit ~$5. Timberline Lodge makes a great base. One of US most accessible backcountry circuits.

name: Toubkal Circuit
keyFeatures: North Africa's highest peak, Berber villages
costNotes: Guide + mule strongly recommended. Berber guesthouses ~DH150/night. Morocco very affordable.

name: Yading Kora
keyFeatures: Sacred peaks, Tibetan pilgrimage, alpine meadows
costNotes: Scenic area entry fee ~¥270 + shuttle. Guesthouses ¥100–200/night. Cheap and spectacular Tibetan plateau.

name: Teton Crest Trail
keyFeatures: Grand Teton skyline, Death Canyon, alpine lakes
costNotes: Backcountry permit ~$45. Grand Teton NP entry ~$35. All food carried — no services on route.

name: Wind River High Route
keyFeatures: Cirque of the Towers, remote wilderness, off-trail navigation
costNotes: Pinedale permit ~$30. Fully self-sufficient — no facilities on route. Resupply cache at Big Sandy Lodge.

name: Sierra High Route
keyFeatures: Off-trail Sierra traverse, Kings Canyon, expert navigation
costNotes: Permits from multiple ranger districts ~$20 each. Expert navigation required. No trails — true off-route travel.

name: Tombstone Territorial Park
keyFeatures: Patagonia of the North, Divide Lake, autumn colour, grizzly habitat
costNotes: Yukon backcountry permit required. Remote — float plane options add cost. Whitehorse flights expensive.

name: Skyline Trail
keyFeatures: Jasper alpine meadows, panoramic ridgeline, Maligne Lake
costNotes: Jasper NP backcountry permit ~CAD$10/night. Short trail, easy access from Jasper townsite.

name: Rockwall Trail
keyFeatures: Kootenay NP, 30km limestone wall, hanging glaciers
costNotes: Kootenay NP backcountry permit ~CAD$10/night. Accessible from highway — low logistics overhead.

name: Choquequirao Trek
keyFeatures: Remote Incan citadel, cloud forest terraces, no crowds
costNotes: Entry fee ~$8. No roads, no crowds — mule support helps. One of Peru's best value secret ruins treks.

name: Quilotoa Loop
keyFeatures: Turquoise volcanic crater lake, indigenous villages, Andes scenery
costNotes: Ecuador uses USD. Guesthouses $10–20/night. Village homestays cheaper. Outstanding budget Andean loop.

name: Mount Roraima Trek
keyFeatures: Ancient tepui plateau, Lost World landscape, triple border summit, endemic species
costNotes: Guide mandatory by regulation. Venezuelan side cheapest; Brazilian side more expensive logistics.

name: Dientes Circuit
keyFeatures: Southernmost trek in the world, Navarino Island, raw wilderness
costNotes: Ferry from Punta Arenas to Puerto Williams required (~$150). World's southernmost circuit — logistics intensive.

name: Cerro Castillo Circuit
keyFeatures: Basalt spires, hanging glaciers, far fewer crowds than Torres
costNotes: CONAF park fee ~$10. Less-visited than Torres del Paine — no booking congestion. Coyhaique is the base.

name: Cape Wrath Trail
keyFeatures: Britain's toughest route, remote Scottish Highlands, no waymarking
costNotes: No marked trail — navigation required. Wild camping free under Scottish access laws. Ferry/minibus across Cape Wrath.

name: Tour of Monte Rosa
keyFeatures: Circumnavigates Monte Rosa massif, Zermatt, Matterhorn views
costNotes: Swiss hut nights €70–100. Italian/French sections cheaper. Switzerland dominates cost.

name: Stubai High Trail
keyFeatures: Tyrol glacier panoramas, superb hut network, classic circuit
costNotes: DAV huts €35–55/night incl. half-board. ÖAV membership gets significant hut discounts. Excellent value Alps.

name: Berliner Höhenweg
keyFeatures: Zillertal Alps traverse, glaciers, classic Austrian hut culture
costNotes: Zillertal huts ~€40–60/night half-board. ÖAV membership recommended. Classic Austrian hut-to-hut.

name: Hornstrandir Traverse
keyFeatures: Iceland's most remote peninsula, arctic foxes, dramatic fjord cliffs
costNotes: Ferry from Ísafjörður ~$100 each way. Iceland expensive — budget $100+/day for food/logistics.

name: Kanchenjunga Base Camp
keyFeatures: World's 3rd highest peak, remote wilderness, dual base camp circuit
costNotes: Restricted Area Permit ~$20/week + park fee. Very remote — plan extra days & budget for delays.

name: Upper Mustang Trek
keyFeatures: Ancient Tibetan kingdom, ochre cliffs, cave monasteries, restricted area
costNotes: Restricted Area Permit $500/10 days — mandatory guide required. Most expensive Nepal trekking area.

name: Makalu Base Camp
keyFeatures: 5th highest peak, pristine Barun valley, true wilderness Nepal
costNotes: Restricted Area Permit ~$20/week + SNP fee. Very remote — teahouses in lower valley, camping higher up.

name: Dhaulagiri Circuit
keyFeatures: French Pass, Hidden Valley, 7th highest peak circuit
costNotes: Technical high route — full camping required. French Pass at 5360m. 14-day permit zone entry fees apply.

name: Chadar Trek
keyFeatures: Walking on frozen Zanskar River, ice caves, Ladakhi winter wilderness
costNotes: Guide + tent + all meals required. Leh to trailhead transport extra. Short window in Jan–Feb only.

name: MacLehose Trail
keyFeatures: 100km across Hong Kong, coastal ridgelines, city skyline backdrop
costNotes: Trail free to use. Camping HKD$12/night. Hong Kong city itself may add costs for accommodation.

name: Mount Meru Trek
keyFeatures: Dramatic crater ridge, Kilimanjaro acclimatisation, wildlife below treeline
costNotes: Park fees ~$450 + guide mandatory. Typically done as Kilimanjaro acclimatisation add-on.

name: Berg Lake Trail
keyFeatures: Mount Robson, Berg Glacier, Emperor Falls, highest Canadian Rockies peak
costNotes: BC Parks reservation fee ~CAD$10/night. Very accessible from Valemount. Outstanding value in the Canadian Rockies.

name: Kepler Track
keyFeatures: Lake Te Anau, Fiordland ridge walk, one of NZ's Great Walks
costNotes: Hut fees ~NZD$80/night. One of NZ's Great Walks. Easy to do independently.

name: Dusky Track
keyFeatures: NZ's most remote and challenging wilderness route, raw Fiordland
costNotes: Floatplane access from Queenstown ~NZD$800 each end. Basic DOC huts ~NZD$15/night. Notoriously demanding.

name: Appalachian Trail
keyFeatures: Georgia to Maine, 3,500km, world's most famous thru-hike, section-hikeable
costNotes: ~$1/mile typical budget thru. Resupply every 3–5 days. Gear up-front cost ~$1500–3000 additional.

name: Pacific Crest Trail
keyFeatures: Mexico to Canada, Sierra Nevada & Cascades, Wild made this famous, section-hikeable
costNotes: ~$1.50–2/mile. Permit required (free but competitive). Sierra resupply by post box or Mammoth/Yosemite.

name: Continental Divide Trail
keyFeatures: Mexico to Canada along the Continental Divide, wildest Triple Crown trail, section-hikeable
costNotes: Least infrastructure of the Triple Crown. Many road walks and alternates. Water sources critical in New Mexico.

name: Te Araroa
keyFeatures: Cape Reinga to Bluff end-to-end, crosses Tongariro & NZ Great Walks, section-hikeable
costNotes: Huts and camping mix. NZ Great Walks section fees extra. Crosses remote Fiordland — floatplane optional.

name: Great Himalaya Trail
keyFeatures: Longest Himalayan traverse, crosses all Nepal mountain regions, world's highest long trail, section-hikeable
costNotes: Multiple restricted area permits required. Full camping, high logistics. One of the world's most expensive thru-hikes.

name: Israel National Trail
keyFeatures: Eilat to Dan full-country traverse, Negev desert to Galilee forest, section-hikeable
costNotes: Cheap country — hostels ₪80–150/night. Water sources reliable. One of world's best-value thru-hikes.

name: Camino Primitivo
keyFeatures: Original pilgrimage route, tougher and wilder than the Frances, mountain crossing
costNotes: Albergues €10–16/night. Budget €25–40/day. Less commercial than Camino Francés — fewer tourist prices.

name: Torres del Paine W Trek
keyFeatures: Granite spires, glaciers, hanging valleys, condors
costNotes: Park entry ~$40. Refugio beds ~$60–80/night incl. meals. Most popular version of Torres del Paine.

name: Via Alpina (Red Trail)
country: Monaco / France / Italy / Switzerland / Austria / Germany / Slovenia
keyFeatures: 8 Alpine countries, 2,500km traverse, Atlantic to Adriatic
costNotes: Traverses 8 countries with expensive Alpine huts. Switzerland/Austria sections most costly. Budget €80–150/day.

name: GR11 (Ruta Transpirenaica)
keyFeatures: Spanish Pyrenees traverse, Atlantic to Mediterranean, 820km
costNotes: Refugios €15–25/night. Spain very affordable — budget €30–50/day. Less crowded than GR10 French side.

name: GR10
keyFeatures: French Pyrenees traverse, Hendaye to Banyuls, 866km
costNotes: Gîtes d'étape €15–25/night incl. dinner. France more expensive than Spanish side but still good value.

name: Wicklow Way
keyFeatures: Rolling hills, monastic ruins, Glendalough valley
costNotes: B&Bs €40–80/night. Starting from Dublin adds travel cost. One of Ireland's most-walked routes.

name: Pennine Way
keyFeatures: England's oldest long-distance path, remote bogs, historic pubs
costNotes: B&Bs £50–90/night. Budget £60–80/day. England's classic long-distance path — well-serviced with pubs.

name: Adlerweg (Eagle Walk)
keyFeatures: Tyrol traverse, 413km, dramatic Alpine scenery
costNotes: Tyrol mountain inns €45–70/night incl. dinner. ÖAV members get hut discounts. Excellent Austrian value.

name: Via Dinarica (White Trail)
terrain: Karst mountains, remote ridges, dense forest
keyFeatures: Western Balkans traverse, cultural immersion, rugged karst
costNotes: Western Balkans extremely affordable — homestays €15–30/night. One of Europe's best-value long trails.

name: Abel Tasman Coast Track
terrain: Coastal, golden sand beaches, native bush
keyFeatures: Golden beaches, tidal crossings, fur seals, turquoise water
costNotes: Hut fees ~NZD$50/night. Water taxis NZD$40–80 each. NZ Great Walk — book ahead in summer.

name: Great Ocean Walk
keyFeatures: Twelve Apostles, shipwrecks, dramatic sea cliffs
costNotes: Camp fees AUD$20–40/night. Self-sufficient camping. Guided eco-lodges ~AUD$400/night — premium option.

name: Langtang Valley Trek
terrain: River valley, rhododendron forest, high alpine
keyFeatures: Tibetan Buddhist culture, yak pastures, closest Himalayan valley to Kathmandu
costNotes: Langtang NP + TIMS ~$50. Teahouses ~$15–25/night. Closest Himalayan valley to Kathmandu — efficient & cheap.

name: Three Passes Trek
terrain: High altitude, glacial moraine, rocky passes
keyFeatures: Renjo La, Cho La, Kongma La — three 5,000m+ passes, Gokyo Lakes, Everest views
costNotes: Sagarmatha NP + TIMS ~$100. Demanding 3-pass route — teahouses throughout the Khumbu region.

name: Bibbulmun Track
terrain: Eucalyptus forest, coastal heath, karri forest
keyFeatures: 1,000km Perth to Albany, endemic flora, massive wilderness distance
costNotes: Hut fees AUD$10–15/night. 1000km trail — split into manageable sections. Perth is the gateway city.

name: Fann Mountains Traverse
terrain: High alpine, rocky passes, glacial lakes
keyFeatures: Turquoise glacial lakes, Soviet-era base camps, remote Central Asian wilderness
costNotes: Donkey hire ~$20/day. Guesthouses ~$15–25/night. Tajikistan very affordable — one of Central Asia's gems.

name: Long Trail
terrain: Muddy forest, steep ridges, summit bogs
keyFeatures: America's oldest long-distance trail, Vermont spine, 438km
costNotes: Green Mountain Club shelters ~$10/night. Vermont's 273-mile thru — great budget New England option.

name: Colorado Trail
terrain: High elevation alpine tundra, rocky ridges
keyFeatures: Sustained high altitude, 782km Denver to Durango, Rocky Mountains
costNotes: Wilderness permit ~$15. Water & resupply at towns every 5–7 days. 500mi Colorado classic thru-hike.

name: Mount Kinabalu
terrain: Jungle, granite rock, alpine summit zone
keyFeatures: Highest peak in Southeast Asia, sunrise summit, extraordinary biodiversity
costNotes: Mandatory guide + Kinabalu Park entry ~RM400–500. Laban Rata hut ~RM250/night. Must book months ahead.

name: Mount Semeru
terrain: Volcanic ash, savanna, alpine crater zone
keyFeatures: Active volcano, highest peak in Java, dramatic ash cone summit
costNotes: Park entry ~Rp100,000/day. Guide hire ~Rp300,000/day. Permit numbers strictly limited — book early.

name: Zion Traverse
terrain: Desert canyon, sandstone mesas, slot canyons
keyFeatures: Slot canyons, red rock mesas, technical desert navigation
costNotes: Zion NP entry ~$35/vehicle. Narrows permits ~$15. Shuttle system handles logistics well — very accessible.

name: Great Divide Trail
terrain: Very remote alpine, dense bush, river crossings
keyFeatures: Canada's most remote thru-hike, Waterton to Kakwa, extreme wilderness navigation
costNotes: Canada's answer to the PCT — 1000km. Very remote sections in BC require resupply planning. ~$100/day.

name: Tre Cime di Lavaredo
terrain: Alpine rock, limestone scree, high meadows
keyFeatures: Iconic three limestone monoliths, Dolomites, world-class photography
costNotes: Rifugio beds €40–60/night. Cable car options available. One of the Dolomites' most iconic short circuits.

name: St. Olavs Way
keyFeatures: Historic pilgrimage Oslo to Trondheim, Dovrefjell crossing
costNotes: Norway expensive — budget NOK700–1200/day. Pilgrimage route with guesthouses and camping. Book ahead.

name: Lofoten Traverse
terrain: Extreme ridges, coastal bogs, Arctic terrain
keyFeatures: Arctic ocean views, dramatic ridgelines, midnight sun, Norse fishing villages
costNotes: Wild camping free in Norway. Airbnb/rorbuer ~NOK800–1500/night in villages. Norway prices are high.

name: Akshayuk Pass
terrain: Arctic tundra, glacial rivers, boulder fields
keyFeatures: Arctic Circle pass, Thor Peak, polar bear country, Baffin Island
costNotes: Charter flight to Pangnirtung ~CAD$700 each way. Remote Arctic logistics. Nunavut parks permit ~CAD$25.

name: Kashmir Great Lakes
terrain: Alpine meadows, high passes, glacial lakes
keyFeatures: 7 alpine lakes, Himalayan meadows, Sonamarg to Naranag
costNotes: Inner Line Permit required. Horsemen ~₹1500/day. Kashmir valley extremely affordable — one of India's gems.

name: Kerry Way
keyFeatures: MacGillycuddy's Reeks, Ring of Kerry, Killarney National Park
costNotes: B&Bs €60–90/night. Ireland not cheap — budget €70–100/day. 200km Kerry Way circles the peninsula.

name: Shikoku Pilgrimage
terrain: Paved roads, forest tracks, mountain paths
keyFeatures: 88 sacred temples, 1,200km Buddhist circuit, henro pilgrimage tradition
costNotes: Temple lodging (shukubo) ~¥6,000–10,000/night incl. meals. 88 temples, 1200km — budget ¥3000–5000/day.

name: Kokoda Track
terrain: Dense jungle, deep mud, steep ridges
keyFeatures: WWII history, extreme humidity and terrain, Owen Stanley Range
costNotes: Guide mandatory by law. Trek fee ~$350 AUD. PNG logistics expensive — flights/transfers add significantly.

name: Otter Trail
terrain: Coastal forest, river crossings, rocky shoreline
keyFeatures: Bloukrans river crossing, rugged Garden Route coast, South Africa's oldest trail
costNotes: SANParks hut fee ~R550/night. Permit strictly limited — book 12 months ahead. South Africa's most sought trail.

name: Druk Path
terrain: Alpine forest, high passes, glacial lakes
keyFeatures: High alpine lakes, remote dzongs, Paro to Thimphu, Bhutan wilderness
costNotes: Bhutan Sustainable Development Fee $200/day + agency mandatory. Guide-only trek. All-inclusive packages.

name: Great Glen Way
terrain: Canal towpaths, forest tracks, loch shores
keyFeatures: Loch Ness, Caledonian Canal, Fort William to Inverness
costNotes: B&Bs £40–80/night. Scotland's most accessible long trail — Fort William to Inverness. Wild camping free.

name: Tahoe Rim Trail
terrain: Alpine lakes, granite ridges, volcanic peaks
keyFeatures: Circumnavigates Lake Tahoe, 265km, Sierra Nevada and Great Basin views
costNotes: Tahoe area camping permits ~$20/night. Self-sufficient circuit — resupply at South Lake Tahoe. Gear-heavy.

name: Kilimanjaro (Lemosho)
terrain: Rainforest, moorland, alpine desert, arctic summit
keyFeatures: Highest success rate route on Kilimanjaro, Shira Plateau, 8-day acclimatisation
costNotes: Guide mandatory. Lemosho 8-day highest summit rate — adds one extra camp day vs Machame. Park fees ~$800+.

name: Australian Alps Walking Track
keyFeatures: 655km Walhalla to Tharwa, extreme remoteness, high-country snow gums
costNotes: Free camping in national parks. Resupply at towns every 5–8 days. Remote — vehicle shuttle logistics needed.

name: Heaphy Track
terrain: Tussock downs, nikau palm coast, dense bush
keyFeatures: Ecological diversity, Kahurangi National Park, NZ West Coast wilderness
costNotes: Hut fees ~NZD$65/night. One of NZ's Great Walks — diverse ecosystems from mountains to palm-lined coast.

name: Mera Peak
terrain: Himalayan, high-altitude glacier, broad snow slopes
keyFeatures: Nepal's highest trekking peak at 6476m, five 8000m peaks visible from summit, remote Hinku valley approach
permits: Required – NMA climbing permit, Hinku Valley restricted area, TIMS
costNotes: NMA climbing permit ~$250. Hinku valley approach through remote Makalu region. Full camping above base camp.

name: Island Peak (Imja Tse)
keyFeatures: 6189m Khumbu summit, Lhotse–Nuptse–Makalu panorama, fixed rope headwall, Everest region approach
permits: Required – NMA climbing permit, Sagarmatha National Park, TIMS
costNotes: NMA climbing permit ~$350 + Sagarmatha NP + TIMS. Guide essential. Usually packaged with EBC trek.

name: Stok Kangri
terrain: High-altitude scree, snow and rock, non-glaciated summit
keyFeatures: India's most popular 6000m peak at 6153m, Ladakh high-desert approach, Indus Valley views, ideal acclimatisation from Leh
permits: Required – IMF/JKSPORTSCOUNCIL peak permit, Stok village entry fee; guide strongly recommended
costNotes: Peak permit + guide mandatory. Leh-based full package ~₹15,000–30,000. India's most popular 6000m climb.

name: Lobuche East
terrain: Himalayan, rocky technical ridge, mixed terrain
keyFeatures: 6119m technical Khumbu summit, exposed rocky ridge with fixed ropes, Everest–Lhotse–Pumori panorama
permits: Required – NMA climbing permit, Sagarmatha National Park, TIMS
costNotes: NMA climbing permit ~$400. Often combined with EBC itinerary. Technical ice and rock above high camp.

name: Pisang Peak
keyFeatures: 6091m on the Annapurna Circuit, classic north and south ridge routes, Annapurna II backdrop, ideal add-on to the circuit
permits: Required – NMA climbing permit, Annapurna Conservation Area, TIMS
costNotes: NMA permit ~$250 + ACAP permit. Add-on to Annapurna Circuit — very efficient in combined package.

name: Huayna Potosí
keyFeatures: World's most accessible 6000m peak at 6088m, La Paz 3-day ascent, glaciated north face, altiplano panorama
permits: Required – guide mandatory, park entry fee
costNotes: World's most accessible 6000m peak. Guided from La Paz ~$300–700. Refugio overnight included.

name: Cotopaxi
keyFeatures: One of the world's highest active volcanoes at 5897m, night ascent on glacier, Cotopaxi National Park, Quito day-trip distance
permits: Required – National Park entry, guide strongly recommended
costNotes: Ecuador uses USD. Guide + park entry ~$300–500 all-in. Popular day/overnight option from Quito.

name: Nevado Pisco
keyFeatures: 5752m above Llanganuco Lakes, Huascarán towering above, Cordillera Blanca benchmark glacier climb
permits: Required – Huascarán National Park entry, guide recommended
costNotes: Huascarán NP entry ~$20. Huaraz-based agencies offer good value guided packages ~$400–700 all-in.

name: Tent Peak (Tharpu Chuli)
keyFeatures: 5663m above Annapurna Sanctuary, Annapurna I–IV encircling the summit, paired naturally with ABC trek
permits: Required – NMA climbing permit, Annapurna Conservation Area, TIMS
costNotes: NMA permit ~$250 + ACAP + TIMS. Paired naturally with Annapurna Sanctuary/ABC itinerary.

name: Mount Elbrus
terrain: Volcanic, high-altitude snowfield, broad glacier plateau
keyFeatures: Europe's highest peak at 5642m, one of the Seven Summits, gondola and snowcat support, iconic Barrels Huts base
permits: Required – border zone permit (Kabardino-Balkaria), guide recommended
costNotes: Barrels Hut overnight ~$80. Gondola/snowcat optional. Accreditation permit ~$50. European highest peak.

name: Pico de Orizaba (Citlaltépetl)
keyFeatures: Third highest peak in North America at 5636m, glaciated Jamapa Glacier route, Aztec sacred mountain, Refugio Piedra Grande overnight at 4260m
permits: Required – CONANP National Park entry, guide strongly recommended; Piedra Grande hut booking essential
costNotes: Park entry ~$10. Piedra Grande hut ~$15/night. Guide ~$200–400/day. Very accessible from Mexico City.

name: Yala Peak
terrain: Himalayan, rocky glaciated summit, moderate snow
keyFeatures: 5500m ideal first Himalayan summit, Langtang valley approach, Kyanjin Gompa base, Shishapangma views across Tibet
permits: Required – NMA climbing permit, Langtang National Park, TIMS
costNotes: NMA permit ~$250 + Langtang NP + TIMS. Ideal first Himalayan summit — shorter and cheaper than Khumbu peaks.

name: Friendship Peak
keyFeatures: 5289m classic beginner alpinism peak above Manali, Beas Kund glacier approach, Kullu valley panorama, India's most accessible glaciated summit
permits: Required – IMF/Himachal Pradesh Mountaineering permit; guide recommended
costNotes: IMF/HP permit ~₹3000. Manali guide + equipment hire ~₹5000/day. One of India's best-value alpine climbs.

name: Mount Kazbek
keyFeatures: 5053m Caucasus peak, Gergeti Trinity Church backdrop, Prometheus mythological mountain, Georgia–Russia border summit
permits: Required – border zone permit, guide recommended
costNotes: Permit ~₾30. Gergeti glacier camping ~₾20. Georgia extremely affordable — one of the best-value 5000m peaks.

name: Gran Paradiso
keyFeatures: Highest peak entirely in Italy at 4061m, Gran Paradiso National Park, ibex-rich approach, historic rifugio
costNotes: Rifugio Vittorio Emanuele €50/night incl. dinner. Guide hire ~€350/day. Italy's most accessible 4000m peak.
